//: DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE before reading the comments below:
This is the default configuration for Tiny Tapeout projects. It should fit most designs.
If you change it, please make sure you understand what you are doing. We are not responsible
if your project fails because of a bad configuration.
!!! DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE unless you know what you are doing !!!
If you get stuck with this config, please open an issue or get in touch via the discord.
Here are some of the variables you may want to change:
PL_TARGET_DENSITY_PCT - You can increase this if Global Placement fails with error GPL-0302.
Users have reported that values up to 80 worked well for them.
CLOCK_PERIOD - Increase this in case you are getting setup time violations.
The value is in nanoseconds, so 40ns == 25MHz. This chip runs at 25MHz
(the full-rate QSPI controller gates SCK from the 25MHz clk); constraining
at the real operating period keeps the resizer from over-buffering a large
8x2 design and hurting routability. Tighten toward 20ns only if wanted.
Hold slack margin. 8x2 is TT's LARGEST tile, and this design is marginal on
it: clk reaches 3553 sinks across the wide 1378x226 (6:1) die with ~1300um
avg leaf wire -> CTS skew -> a post-CTS hold-buffer FLOOD (2723 @ margin
0.1/0.05; 1224-2057 @ 0.05/0.02, placement-noise dependent) that detailed
placement can't legalize (DPL-0036, ~64-98 stuck instances). Base util is
already 0.612, so density is not a lever and a bigger die is not available.
CTS_CLK_MAX_WIRE_LENGTH was tried and had ZERO effect (LibreLane 3.0.3 did
not bind it; avg leaf wire unchanged). The one remaining direct lever on the
failing stage is the post-CTS hold margin: 0.05 -> 0.02 sheds the near-
threshold buffers (real negative-slack holds are still fixed) to try to get
the flood under the legalization limit. If this still fails, the design must
SHRINK (fewer flops/cells) -- config cannot fit it on 8x2.
RUN_LINTER, LINTER_INCLUDE_PDK_MODELS - Disabling the linter is not recommended!
If you need a custom clock configuration, read the following documentation first:
https://tinytapeout.com/faq/#how-can-i-map-an-additional-external-clock-to-one-of-the-gpios
SCK NOTE: uio_out[3] is a GATED clock (sck = sck_en & ~clk in qspi_ctrl.sv)
that drives ONLY the external cartridge pad -- NO internal register is
clocked by it (all storage is posedge/negedge clk). The base SDC already
constrains it as a clk-launched output via set_output_delay [all_outputs],
so no create_generated_clock is required and CTS never tree-balances it.
Configuration docs: https://librelane.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/configuration.html
!!! DO NOT CHANGE ANYTHING BELOW THIS POINT !!!
Save some time
Don't put clock buffers on the outputs
Reduce wasted space
Absolute die size
Clock
Don't generate power rings
MAGIC_DEF_LABELS may cause issues with LVS
Only export pin area in LEF (without any connected nets)
